headline: GLCs paid RM2.8 billion in board fees while 6 reported losses
context: Government-linked companies paid RM2.8 billion in combined board fees and executive compensation in 2025, including at six GLCs that reported net operating losses.

cards.big: RM2.8 billion in GLC board fees — even the 6 money-losing ones paid handsomely
cards.sub: Directors rewarded regardless of company performance with public money

cards.big: GLC Transformation Programme guidelines link pay to performance — on paper
cards.sub: Implementation gap means guidelines are aspirational, not enforced

cards.big: 6 loss-making GLCs paid combined RM340 million in board fees and executive pay
cards.sub: Directors earned RM340 million while their companies destroyed shareholder value

cards.big: GLC remuneration committees have no independent external members in 14 of top 20
cards.sub: Directors set their own pay through committees controlled by other directors

cards.big: Paying directors RM340 million for losing money isn't compensation — it's a reward for failure
Should GLC executive compensation be automatically reduced when companies report losses?
